codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of the dashboard overview with 7 widgets (monthly spending, budget status, recent transactions, net worth trend, spending pie chart, month-over-month comparison) plus month navigation. The data layer (getDashboardData) correctly excludes transfers and income from spending calculations, handles split transactions, computes net worth from account balances, and merges category data across months for comparisons. 16 comprehensive tests cover all data aggregation scenarios including edge cases (empty data, no previous snapshot, split transactions, income exclusion). The UI components handle empty states gracefully with helpful messages. Code is well-organized with clean separation between server component (page.tsx), client component (DashboardClient.tsx), query layer (dashboard.ts), API route, and chart component.

issues.description: Tests only cover the query layer (getDashboardData), not the API route handler (GET /api/dashboard). AGENTS.md says 'tests must cover BOTH the database query layer AND the API route handlers.' The API route has validation logic (month format regex) and error handling that isn't tested. However, this is consistent with the existing test pattern across the project (accounts, budgets, etc. also only test query functions), so the risk is low.

Month parameter validation regex /^\d{4}-\d{2}$/ allows semantically invalid months like '2026-00' or '2026-13'. These won't cause errors (just return empty data), but stricter validation (e.g., checking month 01-12) would be more robust.

The fetchData catch block silently swallows errors without user feedback. When month navigation fails, the UI keeps showing stale data with no indication of failure. While AGENTS.md specifies useToast for 'API mutations' (and this is a read), providing some error feedback would improve UX.

Recent transactions query fetches the 10 most recent transactions globally (across all months), not scoped to the selected month. This is a reasonable design choice for a 'recent' feed, but could be confusing when the user navigates to a historical month and sees transactions from a different time period.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md says 'For features with API endpoints, tests must cover BOTH the database query layer AND the API route handlers', but the dashboard tests (and most other feature tests in the project) only test query functions directly, not API route handlers. The stated convention doesn't match actual project practice.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/__tests__/api/dashboard.test.ts imports getDashboardData directly. Similarly, src/__tests__/api/accounts.test.ts imports query functions (getAllAccountsGrouped, createAccount, etc.) and src/__tests__/api/budgets.test.ts imports (getBudgetsForMonth, upsertBudget, etc.) — none of these test the actual HTTP route handlers.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The architecture.md file lists query files in src/db/queries/ but doesn't include dashboard.ts. The API routes listing also doesn't include /api/dashboard. New routes and query files added by workers aren't reflected in the documented architecture.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md lists queries/ directory with accounts.ts, transactions.ts, budgets.ts, etc. but not dashboard.ts. The api/ routes section lists many routes but not /api/dashboard/.

summary: PASS. The dashboard-overview feature is well-implemented with all 7 required widgets, correct data aggregation (transfers and income excluded from spending), comprehensive empty state handling, and 16 tests covering accuracy and edge cases. No blocking issues found. Non-blocking observations: API route tests are missing (consistent with project pattern), month validation could be stricter, and error handling on month navigation is silent. The implementation correctly handles split transactions in spending breakdowns, computes net worth from current account balances, and provides month-over-month comparison with both current and previous month data.
